part.name: RATE PLATE-PLATE
title_block_notes: Part table lists CM03B364360 with process per AS SHOWN and used on CM03B364361.
Part table lists CM03B364361 with process per PS-135.22 * and used on MB05B764050, MB05B764040.
Bond prep note: BOND PREP ALL AROUND PART *.
Metric dimensions and tolerances conform to ASME Y14.5-2009.
Default tolerances read as X.X +/-0.5, X.XX +/-0.25, angles +/-2 degrees.

evidence.visual_location: bottom-right title block, drawing number field

evidence.cited_text: PART NO. CM03B364360 ... PROCESS PER AS SHOWN ... USED ON CM03B364361; CM03B364361 ... PS-135.22 * ... MB05B764050, MB05B764040
UNITS METRIC; DIMENSIONS & TOLERANCES CONFORM TO ASME Y14.5-2009

evidence.visual_location: center-left main semicircular view, lower left end

evidence.cited_text: Length derived as 2 x R38.49 = 76.98; height interpreted as R38.49 + 1.50 = 39.99 where the 1.50 straight end extension lies below the baseline.

coordinate_system.primary_axis: X across the semicircular span in the main view; Y across the 54.00 mm width shown in SECTION A-A; Z through the sheet thickness/local surface normal, with +Z on the outer/convex formed face.
coordinate_system.orientation_notes: The main view is treated as an X-Z projection of a formed arched plate. SECTION A-A is a Y-Z section through the central cutting plane. The A-A letters are section identifiers, not datum feature symbols.

features.name: Formed arched sheet plate body
features.description: Overall formed sheet-metal plate body with semicircular/arched primary profile, nominal 2.00 mm gage thickness, 54.00 mm transverse width in section, and outer R38.49 formed curvature. The main-view horizontal span is derived from the outer radius as approximately 76.98 mm.

geometry.position_description: Main body formed as an arched plate; overall transverse width 54.00 in SECTION A-A; centerline at 27.0 from side; lower end straight extensions interpreted as 1.50 mm at two places.
geometry.axis_direction: X span, Y width, Z local thickness/convex face normal

features.material_condition: Nominal sheet/gage thickness 2.00 +/- 0.05 mm; metal thickness variation +/-30% allowable in formed areas.

evidence.visual_location: SECTION A-A, top overall width dimension
center-left main view and center-right SECTION A-A

features.name: Outer convex formed radius
features.description: Primary outer/convex curved surface or profile radius called out as R38.49, visible in both the main arched view and SECTION A-A. It defines the major formed curvature of the plate body.

geometry.position_description: Outer arched/convex profile, called out R38.49 in the main view and again in SECTION A-A; interpreted as the same formed outside radius.
geometry.axis_direction: Curved profile across X-Z in main view and across Y-Z in section

features.material_condition: Formed area; thickness variation note applies.

evidence.visual_location: center-left main view, leader to outer arc
SECTION A-A, leader to crown radius

features.name: Two inner arcuate radii
features.description: Pair of inner/parallel arcuate radii in the main semicircular view, called out as 2X R32.30. These define the smaller inner curved edges or inner formed boundaries of the arched plate.

geometry.position_description: Two identical inner arcuate features in the main view, both labeled by the 2X R32.30 callout; likely concentric/parallel to the R38.49 outer profile.

evidence.visual_location: center-left main view, upper right radius callout
center-left main semicircular view, callout to inner arcs

features.name: Two lower end straight lands
features.description: Two short lower end straight/flat lands at the open ends of the arched profile, called out as 2X 1.50. The exact role of this 1.50 dimension is somewhat ambiguous, but it appears to define a straight end extension/land height at both lower ends.

geometry.position_description: Two lower open-end lands of the arched profile, one on each side, labeled 2X 1.50 in the main view.
geometry.axis_direction: At the -X and +X lower ends of the arched profile

evidence.visual_location: center-left main view, lower left dimension
center-left main view, lower left end detail

features.name: Two transverse side lands or flanges
features.description: Two side land/flange regions at the transverse edges of SECTION A-A. The callout is read as 2X 5.5 A/C, interpreted as 5.5 mm side lands or as-cut offsets at both sides of the 54.00 mm section width.

geometry.position_description: Paired side features at the left and right edges of SECTION A-A; each appears to occupy 5.5 mm from the outside edge to the curved crown transition.

dimensional_tolerances.tolerance_class: general X.X +/-0.5; callout text A/C ambiguous

evidence.visual_location: center-right SECTION A-A, upper left callout to side edge

features.name: Two top outside corner radii
features.description: Two R2.0 corner radii at the top outer side corners in SECTION A-A, one at each transverse side edge.

geometry.position_description: Paired small radii at the upper left and upper right side corners of SECTION A-A, blending the side lands/flanges into the adjacent top surfaces.

evidence.visual_location: center-right SECTION A-A, callout to upper corner

features.name: All sharp edges broken 0.5 max
features.description: General edge-break requirement applying to all sharp edges of the part: break all sharp edges 0.5 mm maximum.

geometry.position_description: Applies generally to all sharp external and internal edges unless otherwise radiused.

relationships.detail: The R38.49 outer convex radius is an integral formed/profile feature of the overall plate body.
The two R32.30 inner arcuate radii define inner/profile boundaries of the formed plate body.
The two 1.50 lower end lands terminate and intersect the main arched body at the open ends.
The paired 5.5 side lands/flanges are integral transverse edge features of the body in SECTION A-A.
The two R2.0 corner radii blend or modify the side land/flange top outside corners.
The general 0.5 max edge break modifies all sharp edges of the part body.
The outer R38.49 arc and the 2X R32.30 inner arcs appear to share the same center in the main view.
Symmetric relationship: the 2X R32.30 inner arcs are concentric with the outer R38.49 arc in the main view.
The R2.0 top outside corner radii in SECTION A-A blend into the R38.49 convex crown/outer formed surface.
The lower end lands intersect the inner arcuate radii at the open ends of the arched profile.

datum_scheme.datum_hierarchy_notes: No datum feature symbols were identified. The A-A labels and arrows are a section cutting plane only, not datum identifiers.

sections_and_views.orientation: X-Z projection/front view of the arched formed plate, showing the semicircular profile and the SECTION A-A cutting line.
sections_and_views.notes: Contains radius callouts R38.49 and 2X R32.30, lower-end 2X 1.50 callout, and section arrows A-A.

sections_and_views.orientation: Y-Z cross-section through the central cutting plane, showing 54.00 mm overall transverse width, 27.0 mm centerline, R38.49 crown radius, 2X 5.5 A/C side lands, and 2X R2.0 top corner radii.
sections_and_views.notes: Section view is hatched and labeled SECTION A-A; it defines the transverse section profile.

coating.coverage: General part-level coating note; no separate coating-zone boundary shown.

part_level_specs.general_tolerance_standard: METRIC; dimensions and tolerances conform to ASME Y14.5-2009; default tolerances read as X.X +/-0.5, X.XX +/-0.25, angles +/-2 degrees.

feature_graph.graph_summary: The drawing defines a formed arched sheet plate titled RATE PLATE-PLATE, made from SAE J1392 Grade 1018 steel gage 2.00 +/-0.05 mm. The part is a curved/formed shell with a main semicircular profile using an outer R38.49 radius, paired inner 2X R32.30 radii, two 1.50 lower end lands, and a transverse SECTION A-A width of 54.00 mm centered at 27.0 mm. SECTION A-A shows the formed crown radius R38.49, two 5.5 A/C side land/flange regions, and two R2.0 upper side corner radii. General requirements include allowable +/-30% thickness variation in formed areas, break all sharp edges 0.5 max, phosphate coat per PS-119.0, and a starred bond-prep note associated with the process table. No holes, threads, slots, GD&T frames, or datum feature symbols are visible; A-A is a section cut only.

clarifications.question: Which part-table row is the RFQ intended to cover: CM03B364360 with process per AS SHOWN, or CM03B364361 with PS-135.22 and the starred bond-prep requirement?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The part table shows two related part numbers with different process entries, and the starred bond-prep note may apply only to the PS-135.22 row.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use CM03B364360 / AS SHOWN, matching the RFQ file name, while retaining phosphate coat per PS-119.0 as a general note.

clarifications.question: Confirm the intended interpretation of the SECTION A-A callout '2X 5.5 A/C' and the main-view '2X 1.50' callout: are these side land/as-cut offsets and lower end land heights respectively?
clarifications.why_it_matters: These two dimensions define small edge/land features and affect the detailed formed profile and derived overall height. The letters 'A/C' and the 1.50 feature are legible but geometrically ambiguous in the raster view.
clarifications.suggested_default: Interpret 2X 5.5 A/C as two 5.5 mm side land offsets in SECTION A-A and 2X 1.50 as two lower end straight land heights below the main baseline.

clarifications.question: Confirm the complete drawing number and revision, since the title-block drawing number appears as 'CM03B36436-' and the revision box is not clearly populated.
clarifications.why_it_matters: This affects document traceability, although it does not block geometric feature extraction.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use drawing number CM03B36436- and revision null/blank as read from the title block.

clarifications.question: Please verify feature F005 (Two transverse side lands or flanges). It was extracted with low confidence (0.64). Confirm whether this feature exists and its dimensions are correct.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Low-confidence features may be misread dimensions or hallucinated geometry. Agent 2 needs accurate data to plan machining operations.

guardrail_results.message: Title block, material, units, tolerances, gage, and notes were read first. Drawing-number tail and revision are not fully legible, so metadata confidence is reduced.

Drawing is not ordinate-dimensioned. Envelope was derived from explicit R38.49 semicircular radius, SECTION A-A 54.00 width, and the 2X 1.50 lower land interpretation rather than from unrelated callouts.
Full drawing scan found no M-thread callouts and no internal or external thread features.
Full drawing scan found no diameter-symbol hole callouts and no visible hole features. No numeric 'X' callout without a diameter symbol was treated as a hole.
No slots, pockets, channels, holes, or other depth-controlled cut features are visible; therefore no missing depth values remain.
A-A was identified as a section cut with paired arrows, not a datum feature. No datum feature symbols or GD&T frames are visible.
Every feature node participates in at least one relationship. The only symmetric relationship present, concentricity between R38.49 and R32.30 arcs, is included in both directions.
The '2X 5.5 A/C' callout and detailed meaning of the '2X 1.50' lower-end callout are partially ambiguous; clarification questions were added and feature confidence was reduced.

analysis_trace.summary: Single landscape engineering drawing page reviewed. Main geometry is centered, notes are lower left, part table is upper left, and title block is lower right. The drawing uses third-angle projection and metric units.
issues: Rightmost title-block drawing number/revision area is partially cropped or low legibility.

analysis_trace.summary: Read title block and notes first. Extracted title RATE PLATE-PLATE, material SAE J1392 Grade 1018, gage 2.00 +/-0.05, minimum yield 340 MPa, ASME Y14.5-2009 metric tolerance block, phosphate coat per PS-119.0, break sharp edges 0.5 max, formed-area thickness variation allowance, and bond-prep note.
issues: Part table has two part numbers with different process entries; assumed CM03B364360 / AS SHOWN for the RFQ.

analysis_trace.summary: Identified a main semicircular/arched view at center-left and SECTION A-A at center-right. A-A cutting arrows are section indicators, not datum symbols.

Extracted the formed arched body, outer R38.49 radius, 2X R32.30 inner arcs, 2X 1.50 lower end lands, 54.00 section width, 27.0 centerline, 2X 5.5 A/C side lands, 2X R2.0 top corner radii, and 0.5 max edge break.
issues: The detailed geometric meaning of '2X 5.5 A/C' and the lower '2X 1.50' dimension is not completely certain from the raster image.

analysis_trace.summary: Correlated the R38.49 callout in the main view and SECTION A-A as the same principal formed/convex surface radius. The SECTION A-A features define transverse details of the same formed body rather than separate parts.
issues: The drawing implies a formed shell/plate; exact surface type may be doubly curved or formed in two projections, but no additional view is provided.

analysis_trace.summary: Built a feature graph with the part body as the parent, profile radii and edge lands as attached features, and edge break as a global profile modifier. Added concentric relationship between the main outer and inner arcs.
Embedded explicit gage tolerance 2.00 +/-0.05 and applied title-block default tolerances to visible dimensions based on decimal precision: X.X +/-0.5 and X.XX +/-0.25. No GD&T frames were found.
issues: Default tolerance block is readable but slightly small; confidence remains high enough for extraction.

analysis_trace.summary: Scanned for datum feature symbols and GD&T. No boxed datum letters attached to features were found. A-A is a section cut only, so datum scheme is empty.

Ran thread, hole, depth, dimension, relationship, datum, and envelope checks. No threads, holes, diameter symbols, slots, pockets, or depth symbols are present. All features have relationships and the only symmetric relationship is mirrored. Envelope uses the maximum visible profile radius and section width rather than unrelated callouts.
issues: Envelope height includes an interpretation of the 1.50 lower-end land; clarification C002 records this ambiguity.
